source_name: Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism Standard (ML-KEM)
requirement: Ensure overall implementation provides an acceptable level of security as determined by the responsible authority in each agency or department.
evidence_quote: The responsible authority in each agency or department shall ensure that an overall implementation provides an acceptable level of security.

source_name: Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism Standard (ML-KEM)
requirement: Ensure that any module implementing key establishment capability is designed and built in a secure manner.
evidence_quote: It is the responsibility of the implementer to ensure that any module that implements a key establishment capability is designed and built in a secure manner.

source_name: Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism Standard (ML-KEM)
requirement: Guard against the disclosure of randomness used by parties, decapsulation keys, and shared secret keys to maintain security guarantees.
evidence_quote: Users shall, therefore, guard against the disclosure of these values [randomness used by the two parties, the decapsulation key, and the shared secret key itself].

source_name: Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism Standard (ML-KEM)
requirement: Employ cryptographic algorithms approved for protecting Federal Government-sensitive information in implementations complying with this standard.
evidence_quote: Implementations that comply with this standard shall employ cryptographic algorithms that have been approved for protecting Federal Government-sensitive information.

source_name: Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism Standard (ML-KEM)
requirement: Ensure exports of cryptographic modules implementing this standard comply with federal laws and are licensed by the Bureau of Industry and Security.
evidence_quote: Exports of cryptographic modules that implement this standard and technical data regarding them must comply with all federal laws and regulations and be licensed by the Bureau of Industry and Security of the U.S. Department of Commerce.

source_name: Algorithm Identifiers for ML-DSA in X.509 PKI
requirement: Configure CA implementations to explicitly specify ML-DSA algorithms using the defined OIDs when encoding signatures in certificates and CRLs.
evidence_quote: Conforming Certification Authority (CA) implementations MUST specify the algorithms explicitly by using the OIDs specified in Section 2 when encoding ML-DSA signatures in certificates and CRLs.

source_name: Algorithm Identifiers for ML-DSA in X.509 PKI
requirement: Ensure client implementations recognize the specific OIDs defined for ML-DSA when processing certificates and CRLs.
evidence_quote: Conforming client implementations that process certificates and CRLs using ML-DSA MUST recognize the corresponding OIDs.

source_name: Algorithm Identifiers for ML-DSA in X.509 PKI
requirement: Enforce that the keyUsage extension in certificates containing ML-DSA public keys includes at least one of: digitalSignature, nonRepudiation, keyCertSign, or cRLSign.
evidence_quote: If the keyUsage extension is present in a certificate that includes id-ml-dsa-* ... then the subject public key can only be used for verifying digital signatures ... This means that the keyUsage extension MUST have at least one of the following bits set: digitalSignature, nonRepudiation, keyCertSign, cRLSign

source_name: Algorithm Identifiers for ML-DSA in X.509 PKI
requirement: Prohibit the use of keyEncipherment, dataEncipherment, keyAgreement, encipherOnly, or decipherOnly bits in the keyUsage extension for certificates containing ML-DSA public keys.
evidence_quote: ML-DSA subject public keys cannot be used to establish keys or encrypt data, so the keyUsage extension MUST NOT have any of the following bits set: keyEncipherment, dataEncipherment, keyAgreement, encipherOnly, decipherOnly

source_name: Algorithm Identifiers for ML-DSA in X.509 PKI
requirement: Adopt the seed format for encoding ML-DSA private keys to efficiently store both private and public key components.
evidence_quote: The seed format is RECOMMENDED as it efficiently stores both the private and public key.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Identify cryptographic algorithms and cipher suites in use across protocols, applications, and infrastructure to enable transitions.
evidence_quote: Security protocols include a mechanism to identify the algorithm or cipher suite in use. An algorithm identifier might be explicitly carried in the protocol, a management mechanism can be used to identify the algorithm, or the algorithm might be implied by the protocol version number.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Establish local policies to select algorithms and limit allowable combinations during session establishment.
evidence_quote: Local policy can limit the allowable combinations. ... Local policy may select an algorithm other than the mandatory-to-implement one.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Develop a strategic plan to manage cryptographic risks and integrate crypto agility into the organization's risk management framework.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Utilize certificate expiration and revocation mechanisms to enforce the transition away from vulnerable algorithms.
evidence_quote: A cryptographic key is associated with a particular algorithm, which means that key expiration and revocation are important tools for cryptographic algorithm transition.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Configure systems to prohibit the use of specific vulnerable algorithms to force transitions.
evidence_quote: Additionally, administrators can change a configuration setting to prohibit the use of a particular algorithm as a means of forcing algorithm transition.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Implement mechanisms to determine when deployed implementations have shifted from old algorithms to new ones.
evidence_quote: Implementations should also provide a way to determine when deployed implementations have shifted from the old algorithms to more desirable ones.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Monitor cryptographic research results to discover new attacks and assess impacts on existing protocols.
evidence_quote: Monitoring cryptographic research results provides a way to discover new attacks, assess impacts to existing security protocols, and foresee needed changes.

source_name: Considerations for Achieving Cryptographic Agility
requirement: Ensure algorithm negotiation mechanisms include integrity protection to prevent downgrade attacks.
evidence_quote: The mechanism that a security protocol uses to perform cryptographic algorithm negotiation should include integrity protection. If the integrity of algorithm selection during negotiation is not protected, the protocol will be subject to a downgrade attack...

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Include identification of the root of trust for assets relying on specific trust or credential management frameworks.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Appoint a single manager with responsibility for compiling the inventory who reports to the migration planning manager.
evidence_quote: Appointment of a migration inventory manager: A single manager should be appointed with responsibility for compiling the inventory. The migration inventory manager should report to the migration planning manager.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Allocate budget for inventory compilation acknowledging potential significant financial and organizational costs.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Create a migration plan that includes a full inventory and specifies whether each asset will be migrated or retired.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Plan for new Public Key Certificates containing Quantum Safe public keys for every entity in the PKI.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Ensure the migration plan addresses support for both classical and Quantum Safe algorithms if backwards compatibility is required.
evidence_quote: If backwards compatibility is required during a phased migration, then the application will have to support both classical and Quantum Safe algorithms.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Identify PKI entities unable to handle Quantum Safe primitives and mark them for replacement.
evidence_quote: The planning should identify the ability of any PKI entity to handle certificates containing the larger Quantum Safe public keys and signatures. If any PKI entity cannot handle QSC primitives these entities should be marked for replacement.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Consider provisions for cryptographic agility in new or updated PKI entities to allow switching algorithms if vulnerabilities are found.
evidence_quote: Provisions for cryptographic agility should be considered for any new or updated entities in the PKI: If a vulnerability is found in the Quantum Safe algorithm, it may be necessary to switch to a different Quantum Safe algorithm entirely

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Include dependency testing in the migration plan to verify inter-asset relationships.
evidence_quote: The migration plan should stress the role of dependency testing.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Migrate Hardware Based Security Environments to support Quantum Safe algorithms before migrating dependent assets.
evidence_quote: If any asset to be migrated is dependent on an HBSE, then that HBSE should be migrated to ensure it supports the algorithm and cryptographic key requirements of the migrated asset before the depending asset is migrated.

source_name: Migration Strategies and Recommendations for Quantum-Safe Schemes
requirement: Maintain security state during migration by not removing non-Quantum Safe encryption prior to imposing Quantum Safe encryption.
evidence_quote: Where the base state is that all data is encrypted... the non-QSC encryption should not be removed (i.e. go clear) prior to imposing the QSC based encryption mode.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Acknowledge and accept residual risk when conformance to the standard alone is insufficient to ensure module security.
evidence_quote: While the security requirements specified in this standard are intended to maintain the security provided by a cryptographic module, conformance to this standard is not sufficient to ensure that a particular module is secure... any residual risk is acknowledged and accepted.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140A — CMVP Documentation Requirements
requirement: Provide vendor documentation meeting minimum validation authority requirements for cryptographic modules undergoing independent verification.
evidence_quote: The vendor shall provide documentation for a cryptographic module that fulfils but is not limited to the minimum documentation requirements as specified in the validation authority requirement and management documentation.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140A — CMVP Documentation Requirements
requirement: Verify that vendors provide documentation fulfilling minimum validation authority requirements for cryptographic modules.
evidence_quote: The tester shall verify that the vendor provides documentation for a cryptographic module that fulfils but is not limited to the minimum documentation requirements as specified in the validation authority requirement and management documentation.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140B: CMVP Security Policy Requirements
requirement: Modify, add, or delete Vendor Evidence and Test Evidence as specified by the Validation Authority to ensure compliance with ISO/IEC standards.
evidence_quote: As a validation authority, the Cryptographic Module Validation Program (CMVP) may modify, add, or delete Vendor Evidence (VE) and/or Test Evidence (TE) specified under paragraph 6.14 of the ISO/IEC 24759

source_name: NIST SP 800-140B: CMVP Security Policy Requirements
requirement: Specify the order of the security policy content as required by ISO/IEC 19790 Annex B for cryptographic module validation.
evidence_quote: SP 800-140B also specifies the content of the tabular and graphical information required in ISO/IEC 19790 Annex B... and specify the order of the security policy as specified in ISO/IEC 19790:2012 B.1.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140E — CMVP Approved Authentication Mechanisms
requirement: Ensure operator authentication mechanisms for cryptographic modules meet FIPS 140-3 security level requirements as defined in Table 1.
evidence_quote: Except at level 1, operator authentication acceptance is required to be performed by the module or by the Operating Environment as defined in ISO/IEC 19790.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140E — CMVP Approved Authentication Mechanisms
requirement: Validate that authentication mechanisms align with SP 800-63B normative sections for authenticator types, lifecycle management, and session management.
evidence_quote: Vendors should use SP 800-63B as a framework for authentication requirements... Normative SP 800-63B sections include Section 5, Detailed requirements specific to each type of authenticator; Section 6, Lifecycle management; Section 7, Session Management;

source_name: NIST SP 800-140E — CMVP Approved Authentication Mechanisms
requirement: Require vendors to provide justification when SP 800-63B requirements cannot be met for authentication mechanisms.
evidence_quote: Vendors should use SP 800-63B as a framework for authentication requirements and should provide justification whenever SP 800-63B requirements cannot be met.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140E — CMVP Approved Authentication Mechanisms
requirement: Mandate that testers review and affirm vendor documentation regarding authentication mechanisms.
evidence_quote: Testers should review and affirm the vendor documentation.

source_name: NIST Automated Cryptographic Validation Protocol (ACVP)
requirement: Submit cryptographic algorithm implementations to NVLAP-accredited laboratories for testing via Production ACVTS to obtain validation certificates.
evidence_quote: The Production ACVTS server is only available to NVLAP-accredited testing laboratories, and is the only way to create algorithm validation certificates listed on the Algorithm Validation Page.

source_name: NIST Automated Cryptographic Validation Protocol (ACVP)
requirement: Ensure algorithm implementations successfully complete the cryptographic algorithm validation process to be listed as Approved security functions on FIPS 140 module validation certificates.
evidence_quote: For an algorithm implementation to be listed on a cryptographic module validation certificate as an Approved security function, the algorithm implementation must meet all the requirements of FIPS 140 and must successfully complete the cryptographic algorithm validation process.
evidence_location: Relationship of Algorithm Validation to FIPS 140 Module Validation

source_name: NIST Automated Cryptographic Validation Protocol (ACVP)
requirement: Adhere to management activities and specific responsibilities defined in the CAVP Management Manual for Validation Authorities, laboratories, and vendors.
evidence_quote: The CAVP Management Manual provides effective guidance for the CAVP Validation Authorities, CST laboratories, and vendors who participate in the program. It outlines the management activities and specific responsibilities of the various participating groups

source_name: NIST Automated Cryptographic Validation Protocol (ACVP)
requirement: Maintain a validated inventory of cryptographic algorithm implementations including vendor, implementation details, operational environment, and validation date.
evidence_quote: An algorithm implementation successfully tested by a lab and validated by NIST is added to an appropriate validation list , which identifies the vendor, implementation, operational environment, validation date and algorithm details.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Policy Letter 26
requirement: Establish policy prohibiting evaluation of products banned by statute or executive order for National Security Systems.
evidence_quote: products evaluated under the NIAP for use on or protection of NSS and information contained therein may only include products that are not otherwise prohibited from use on an NSS by statute, executive order, or other directives applicable to NSS owners.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Policy Letter 26
requirement: Reserve the right to refuse evaluation and certification for products subject to acquisition prohibitions.
evidence_quote: In instances where a product will soon be subject to a prohibition from acquisition for use on NSS or has already been prohibited from a class of NSS, NIAP reserves the right to refuse evaluation and certification of the product.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Policy Letter 26
requirement: Require vendors to contact NIAP prior to contracting with a CCTL if affected by supply chain limitations.
evidence_quote: Vendors concerned that they may be affected by this limitation are encouraged to contact NIAP prior to contracting with a CCTL for evaluation under the NIAP Common Criteria Evaluation and Validation Scheme (CCEVS).

source_name: Common Criteria 2022 Part 3 — Security Assurance Components (R1)
requirement: Define measurable life-cycle models for the TOE development process to ensure repeatable assurance.
evidence_quote: ALC_LCD.2 Measurable life-cycle model: The developer shall define a measurable life-cycle model for the TOE development.

source_name: Common Criteria 2022 Part 3 — Security Assurance Components (R1)
requirement: Implement systematic flaw remediation procedures to address identified vulnerabilities in the TOE.
evidence_quote: ALC_FLR.3 Systematic flaw remediation: The developer shall establish and maintain a procedure for the systematic identification and remediation of flaws.

source_name: Common Criteria 2022 Part 3 — Security Assurance Components (R1)
requirement: Utilize a Configuration Management system to control changes to the TOE implementation representation.
evidence_quote: ALC_CMC.2 Use of the CM system: The developer shall use a CM system to manage the TOE development and maintenance.

source_name: Common Criteria 2022 Part 3 — Security Assurance Components (R1)
requirement: Establish authorization controls within the Configuration Management system to restrict changes.
evidence_quote: ALC_CMC.3 Authorization controls: The developer shall establish and enforce authorization controls for the CM system.

source_name: Common Criteria 2022 Part 3 — Security Assurance Components (R1)
requirement: Ensure the Configuration Management system covers the implementation representation of the TOE.
evidence_quote: ALC_CMS.3 Implementation representation CM coverage: The developer shall ensure that the CM system covers the implementation representation of the TOE.

source_name: Common Criteria 2022 Part 3 — Security Assurance Components (R1)
requirement: Define developer-defined life-cycle processes to establish ownership and policy for TOE development.
evidence_quote: ALC_LCD.1 Developer defined life-cycle processes: The developer shall define the life-cycle processes used in the development of the TOE.

source_name: Common Evaluation Methodology (CEM) 2022 R1
requirement: Define and document the responsibilities of evaluation roles including sponsors, evaluators, and certifiers to ensure clear ownership.
evidence_quote: Responsibilities of the roles... Relationship of roles...

source_name: Common Evaluation Methodology (CEM) 2022 R1
requirement: Establish documented policies for the management of evaluation evidence and outputs to ensure traceability.
evidence_quote: Management of evaluation evidence sub-task... Management of evaluation outputs...

source_name: Common Evaluation Methodology (CEM) 2022 R1
requirement: Enforce consistent evaluation processes across all assurance classes (APE, ACE, ASE, ADV, AGD, ALC, ATE, AVA, ACO) to ensure repeatable outcomes.
evidence_quote: This document defines the minimum actions to be performed by an evaluator in order to conduct a CC evaluation...

source_name: EU Cybersecurity Certification Agreed Cryptographic Mechanisms v2.0
requirement: Verify that protection profiles and ICT products rely on agreed cryptographic mechanisms defined in ACM v2 during EUCC evaluation.
evidence_quote: When evaluating protection profiles and ICT products under the EUCC scheme, evaluators should verify that these protection profiles and ICT products preferably rely on agreed cryptographic mechanisms as defined in ACM v2 to provide the security services evaluated under this scheme.

source_name: EU Cybersecurity Certification Agreed Cryptographic Mechanisms v2.0
requirement: Consider using agreed cryptographic mechanisms defined in ACM v2 when deciding on cryptographic protection for ICT products submitted to EUCC certification.
evidence_quote: When deciding which cryptographic mechanisms should cover their need for cryptographic protection... developers of protection profiles and developers of ICT products should consider using the agreed cryptographic mechanisms as defined in ECCG Agreed Cryptographic Mechanisms version 2

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Enforce TLS 1.2 deprecation by end of 2031 and migrate to TLS 1.3 or quantum-safe mechanisms.
evidence_quote: TLS 1.2 is only recommended until the end of 2031, since, based on current knowledge, no quantum-safe key agreement mechanisms will be standardized for TLS 1.2.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Discontinue use of DSA and DHE cipher suites by end of 2029.
evidence_quote: Adjustment of the periods of use, discontinuation of DSA and of DHE cipher suite recommendations from the end of 2029

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Discontinue RSA signatures with PKCS #1 v1.5 padding by end of 2025.
evidence_quote: The use of the signature algorithm rsa (IANA no. 1) is recommended only up to 2025, because it uses the PKCS #1 v1.5 padding scheme

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Delete ephemeral keys irrevocably after use to ensure Perfect Forward Secrecy.
evidence_quote: If a cipher suite with Perfect Forward Secrecy is used, it must be ensured that all ephemeral keys are deleted irrevocably after they have been used and that no copies of these keys were made.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Store private cryptographic keys in certified hardware such as HSMs or chip cards.
evidence_quote: Secure storage of the keys can be achieved, for example by using certified hardware (chip card, HSM).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Disable TLS data compression to prevent CRIME side-channel attacks.
evidence_quote: If this cannot be ensured, it is recommended not to use TLS data compression.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Disable the Heartbeat extension to prevent memory disclosure vulnerabilities.
evidence_quote: It is recommended not to use the Heartbeat extension.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Implement the Encrypt-then-MAC extension to mitigate padding oracle attacks.
evidence_quote: The use of the TLS extension 'encrypt-then-MAC' according to [RFC 7366] is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Implement the Extended Master Secret extension to prevent triple handshake attacks.
evidence_quote: Using the TLS extension Extended Master Secret according to [RFC 7627] is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Maintain an inventory of supported TLS versions and cipher suites aligned with BSI recommendations.
evidence_quote: The following TLS versions are recommended: TLS 1.3 ... Use up to 2032+; TLS 1.2 ... Use up to 2031

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Inventory key lengths ensuring minimum 3000 bits for RSA/DSA and 250 bits for ECDSA/ECDH.
evidence_quote: It is recommended to use at least the following key lengths: ... RSA 3000 Bit ... ECDSA 250 Bit

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Define SA lifetimes based on security requirements and enforce rekeying before expiration.
evidence_quote: The SA lifetime should be defined depending on the security requirements of the application. This applies to both IKE-SAs and IPsec-SAs. In ordinary operating scenarios, the IKE-SA lifetime should not exceed 24 h and the IPsec-SA lifetime should not exceed 4 h.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Renegotiate security associations after the defined period of time or data volume limit has expired.
evidence_quote: Both an IKE-SA and an IPsec-SA should only be valid for a limited period of time and renegotiated after this period of time has expired. As an alternative, the transmitted data volume can also be used as criterion for the renegotiation of an IPsec-SA.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Irrevocably delete ephemeral keys immediately after use and ensure no copies are made.
evidence_quote: If an IKEv2/IPsec connection is secured by means of an encryption algorithm, it must be ensured that all ephemeral keys are deleted irrevocably after they have been used and that no copies of these keys were made.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Store private cryptographic keys in certified hardware to prevent copying, misuse, and manipulation.
evidence_quote: Private cryptographic keys, especially static keys and signature keys, must be stored and processed in a secure manner. This includes, among other things, the protection against copying, misuse and manipulation of the keys. Secure storage of the keys can be achieved, for example by using certified hardware (chip card, HSM).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Migrate from sole use of classical key agreement mechanisms to hybrid quantum-safe mechanisms by end of 2031.
evidence_quote: The sole use of classic key agreement mechanisms is only recommended until the end of 2031... For quantum-safe key agreement, the BSI intends to recommend the mechanisms ml-kem-768 and ml-kem-1024... combined with a key exchange mechanism from Table 6 (hybrid).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Define security policies for SA lifetimes and data volume limits as part of the system administration.
evidence_quote: Indicating binding periods of validity or an upper limit for the data volume is part of a security policy and must be defined by the system administrator.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Define requirements for security associations beforehand by a security administrator.
evidence_quote: With the IKEv2 protocol, a SA can be negotiated. The requirements for this must be defined beforehand by a security administrator.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Consult experts before using SA lifetimes longer than standard recommendations for special scenarios.
evidence_quote: For special scenarios, longer SA lifetimes can be used after consultation with an expert.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Identify possible side channels and implement countermeasures by involving experts.
evidence_quote: Therefore, the developer should identify possible side channels by involving experts in this field and implement corresponding countermeasures.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Ensure the final Evaluation Technical Report is signed by the project manager and Quality Management Officer to attest independence and process adherence.
evidence_quote: The final version of the ETR ... is signed by name by the project manager responsible for the evaluation project or the ITSEF manager ... and by the Quality Management Officer (German: QMB) of the ITSEF

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Document all individuals involved in the evaluation with their perceived roles within the Evaluation Technical Report.
evidence_quote: Evaluation Technical Reports (ETR) contain the names and perceived roles of the individuals involved in the preparation of the report and the results presented therein.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Define and document the specific wording for signatures to guarantee evaluator independence and impartiality.
evidence_quote: Wording for the signature of the project manager / ITSEF manager: 'All the individuals involved in this evaluation are listed in their assumed roles. The independence and impartiality of the evaluators was guaranteed...'

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Document any deviations from approved ITSEF processes and assign responsibility to the management.
evidence_quote: Wording for the signature of the QMB: '...any deviations were individually documented and are the responsibility of the management of the ITSEF.'

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Archive all evidence relevant to the evaluation for the period of validity of the certificate plus three years.
evidence_quote: Archive all the evidence relevant to the evaluation and relating to the procedure the evaluated product for the period of validity of the certificate plus 3 years

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Maintain an audit trail for document revisions by submitting versions with visible change markers alongside clean versions.
evidence_quote: Any updates or additions to the ETR shall be identified, e.g. by inserting change markers. The PDF documents delivered shall each contain a variant with visible change markers and a 'clean' version...

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Notify the certification body immediately of any substantial findings representing significant security risks or deviations preventing certification.
evidence_quote: The ITSEF shall notify the certification body (CB) of any substantial findings. ... A finding is considered important if it meets at least one of the following criteria: The ITSEF considers that the finding represents a significant security risk...

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Enable the certification body to monitor individual evaluation steps on the ITSEF premises where necessary.
evidence_quote: The ITSEF shall enable the certification body (CB) to monitor individual evaluation steps on the premises of the ITSEF where necessary.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Assess Impact Analysis Reports and coordinate test efforts for re-evaluations or recertifications.
evidence_quote: For re-evaluations or recertifications: assess the Impact Analysis Report (IAR) ... Presentation and coordination of the test effort

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Ensure reused Evaluation Technical Reports for recertification include all valid results and clearly identify new versus previous evaluation results.
evidence_quote: If an ETR is reused during a recertification/re-evaluation process, the ETR submitted will be viewed as a new document and shall include all the results of evaluations that are valid for the re-evaluated TOE. The corresponding ETR with change markers shall clearly identify which evaluation results are new...

source_name: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
requirement: Conduct implementation validation testing by an independent accredited party to ensure conformance with DRBG specifications.
evidence_quote: Implementation Testing for Validation: Testing by an independent and accredited party to ensure that an implementation of this Recommendation conforms to the specifications of this Recommendation.

source_name: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
requirement: Perform health testing immediately prior to or during normal operation to verify the implementation continues to perform as validated.
evidence_quote: Health Testing: Testing within an implementation immediately prior to or during normal operation to determine that the implementation continues to perform as implemented and as validated.

source_name: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
requirement: Validate that the entropy source used for DRBG seeding conforms to SP 800-90B requirements.
evidence_quote: Approved entropy source: An entropy source that has been validated as conforming to [SP 800-90B].

source_name: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
requirement: Implement health tests for the Instantiate, Generate, Reseed, and Uninstantiate functions to detect operational failures.
evidence_quote: 11.3 Health Testing... 11.3.2 Testing the Instantiate Function... 11.3.3 Testing the Generate Function... 11.3.4 Testing the Reseed Function... 11.3.5 Testing the Uninstantiate Function

source_name: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
requirement: Define and handle errors encountered during normal operation and health testing to ensure system integrity.
evidence_quote: 11.4 Error Handling... 11.4.1 Errors Encountered During Normal Operation... 11.4.2 Errors Encountered During Health Testing

source_name: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
requirement: Reseed the DRBG mechanism to acquire additional bits that affect the internal state before the seedlife expires.
evidence_quote: Reseed: To acquire additional bits that will affect the internal state of the DRBG mechanism.

source_name: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
requirement: Uninstantiate the DRBG to terminate the instantiation and securely clear internal state.
evidence_quote: Uninstantiate: The termination of a DRBG instantiation.

source_name: SP 800-90A Rev. 1: Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators
requirement: Ensure conformance testing for DRBG implementations is conducted within the CMVP and CAVP frameworks.
evidence_quote: Conformance testing for implementations of this Recommendation will be conducted within the framework of the Cryptographic Module Validation Program (CMVP) and the Cryptographic Algorithm Validation Program (CAVP).

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Submit entropy sources to NVLAP-accredited laboratories for validation testing against SP 800-90B requirements before deployment.
evidence_quote: Validation consists of testing by an NVLAP-accredited laboratory against the requirements of SP 800-90B, followed by a review of the results by CAVP and CMVP.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Collect a sequential dataset of at least 1,000,000 raw samples from the noise source for validation testing.
evidence_quote: A sequential dataset of at least 1 000 000 sample values obtained directly from the noise source (i.e., raw data) shall be collected for validation.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Collect a conditioned sequential dataset of at least 1,000,000 consecutive outputs if a non-listed conditioning component is used.
evidence_quote: If the entropy source includes a conditioning component that is not listed in Section 3.1.5.1.1, a conditioned sequential dataset of at least 1 000 000 consecutive conditioning component outputs shall be collected for validation.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Perform restart tests by restarting the entropy source 1000 times and collecting 1000 consecutive samples for each restart.
evidence_quote: For the restart tests (see Section 3.1.4), the entropy source must be restarted 1000 times; for each restart, 1000 consecutive samples shall be collected directly from the noise source.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Provide rationale and analysis to support any claim that the noise source generates Independent and Identically Distributed (IID) samples.
evidence_quote: The submitter makes an IID claim on the noise source, based on the submitter’s analysis of the design. The submitter shall provide rationale for the IID claim.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Provide an initial entropy estimate for the noise source outputs based on the submitter's analysis of the design.
evidence_quote: The submitter shall provide an entropy estimate for the noise source outputs, which is based on the submitter’s analysis of the noise source.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Implement health tests to ensure the noise source and entropy source continue to operate as expected and detect failures quickly.
evidence_quote: Health tests are an integral part of the entropy source design that are intended to ensure that the noise source and the entire entropy source continue to operate as expected. When testing the entropy source, the end goal is to obtain assurance that failures of the entropy source are caught quickly and with a high probability.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Include health tests capable of detecting likely failure modes for the entropy source and specifically the noise source.
evidence_quote: Another aspect of health testing strategy is determining the likely failure modes for the entropy source and, in particular, for the noise source. Health tests are expected to include tests that can detect these failure conditions.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Categorize health tests into start-up, continuous, and on-demand tests to monitor entropy source status.
evidence_quote: The health tests can be separated into three categories: start-up tests, continuous tests (primarily on the noise source), and on-demand tests.

source_name: SP 800-90B: Recommendation for the Entropy Sources Used for Random Bit Generation
requirement: Ensure the entropy source design accounts for variations in noise source behavior that may affect the entropy rate of the output.
evidence_quote: The developer should consider how the conditioning component to be used and how variations in the behavior of the noise source may affect the entropy rate of the output.

source_name: SP 800-90C: Recommendation for Random Bit Generator (RBG) Constructions
requirement: Validate RBG constructions against SP 800-90C using accredited testing labs to ensure conformance with specified requirements.
evidence_quote: The term 'shall' is used when a requirement is testable by a testing lab during implementation validation using operational tests or a code review.

source_name: SP 800-90C: Recommendation for Random Bit Generator (RBG) Constructions
requirement: Ensure RBG constructions use entropy sources validated for compliance with SP 800-90B or SP 800-90C.
evidence_quote: Each RBG includes a DRBG from SP 800-90A and is based on the use of a randomness source that is validated for compliance with SP 800-90B or SP 800-90C.

source_name: SP 800-90C: Recommendation for Random Bit Generator (RBG) Constructions
requirement: Implement health testing for RBG components to detect failures in entropy sources and non-entropy-source components.
evidence_quote: Section 8 discusses health and implementation validation testing. ... Handling Failures ... Entropy-Source Failures ... Failures by Non-Entropy-Source Components

source_name: SP 800-90C: Recommendation for Random Bit Generator (RBG) Constructions
requirement: Define and enforce reseed procedures for RBG constructions to maintain security strength over time.
evidence_quote: Sections 4, 5, 6, and 7 specify the RBG constructions... Reseeding an RBG2 Construction... Reseeding the DRBG Instantiation...

source_name: SP 800-90C: Recommendation for Random Bit Generator (RBG) Constructions
requirement: Verify that RBG constructions meet minimum entropy requirements for seed material to ensure cryptographic security.
evidence_quote: SP 800-90C requires security_strength/2 bits of randomness to be obtained from a randomness source in place of a nonce in addition to the randomness required to establish a DRBG's security strength.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Conduct annual management reviews of the quality system and policy to ensure continued effectiveness.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Require all personnel performing work for NIAP to sign non-disclosure agreements regarding proprietary information.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)
requirement: Monitor the performance of participating Common Criteria Testing Laboratories (CCTLs) for adherence to scheme terms.

source_name: NIAP CCEVS Quality Manual (Scheme Publication #2)

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Define a progressive transition strategy for cryptographic products to adopt quantum-resistant cryptography.
evidence_quote: L'ANSSI encourage toutes les industries à définir une stratégie de transition progressive vers une cryptographie résistante aux attaques quantiques pour les produits cryptographiques concernés.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Include the quantum threat in risk analysis and consider quantum protection measures for relevant cryptographic products.
evidence_quote: L'ANSSI encourage toutes les industries à inclure la menace quantique dans leur analyse de risque et à envisager d'inclure des mesures de protection quantique dans les produits cryptographiques concernés.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Mandate hybridation for products requiring post-quantum protection, except for specific hash-based signatures.
evidence_quote: L'ANSSI insiste toujours fortement sur la nécessité de l'hybridation partout où une protection post-quantique est nécessaire, à la fois à court et à moyen terme.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Dimension symmetric primitives to ensure post-quantum security levels equivalent to AES-256 and SHA2-384.
evidence_quote: l'ANSSI encourage également à dimensionner les paramètres des primitives symétriques de manière à assurer une sécurité post-quantique conjecturée ; en pratique, au moins le même niveau de sécurité que l'AES-256 pour les algorithmes de chiffrement par bloc et au moins le même niveau de sécurité que SHA2-384 pour les fonctions de hachage.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Avoid modifying normalized parameters for post-quantum algorithms like CRYSTALS-Kyber and CRYSTALS-Dilithium.
evidence_quote: Il est important d'éviter de modifier les paramètres de l'instance normalisée.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Use the highest possible NIST security level (preferably Level 5 or 3) for post-quantum algorithms.
evidence_quote: Nous recommandons d'utiliser le niveau de sécurité NIST le plus élevé possible, de préférence le niveau 5 (équivalent à l'AES-256) ou le niveau 3 (équivalent à l'AES-192).

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Protect the internal state of signature algorithms like XMSS and LMS against integrity attacks and replay.
evidence_quote: L'état interne de l'algorithme de signature est une donnée très critique et doit être protégé en intégrité. Il doit également être protégé contre les attaques par rejeu.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Undergo ANSSI security visa evaluation including mandatory hybridation analysis for post-quantum products.
evidence_quote: Dans la deuxième phase, les tâches d'évaluation cryptographique des visas de sécurité comprennent une analyse de tous les algorithmes cryptographiques, y compris les algorithmes post-quantiques avec une hybridation obligatoire.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Ensure final products implementing post-quantum protection use hybridation unless using specific hash-based signatures.
evidence_quote: Dans ce cas, tout produit qui inclut une protection post-quantique doit mettre en œuvre l'hybridation, sauf dans les rares cas où la protection post-quantique repose uniquement sur des signatures basées sur le hachage comme XMSS, LMS ou SPHINCS+, pour lesquelles l'hybridation est facultative.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Implement hybridation modes using validated standards or well-studied modes with security proofs.
evidence_quote: De manière générale, comme pour toute fonction cryptographique, l'ANSSI recommande d'utiliser des normes ou des modes bien étudiés avec des preuves de sécurité validées.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Prioritize hybrid post-quantum protection for products offering long-term confidentiality beyond 2030.
evidence_quote: L'utilisation d'une protection hybride post-quantique est recommandée en particulier pour les produits de sécurité destinés à offrir une protection durable des informations (jusqu'à après 2030) ou qui seront potentiellement utilisés après 2030 sans mise à jour.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Enforce key re-exchange after one hour or one gigabyte of data transmitted to renew session keys.
evidence_quote: It is recommended to re-exchange the keys according to Chapter 9 in [RFC 4253], i.e. the session keys are renewed after one hour or after one gigabyte has been transmitted (whichever happens first).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Define maximum periods of use for cryptographic mechanisms and discontinue usage after the specified year.
evidence_quote: The recommendations in this Technical Guideline have a maximum period of use. The indication of the year means that the respective mechanism can be used until the end of the year stated.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Plan migration to quantum-safe key agreement mechanisms by the end of 2031.
evidence_quote: The sole use of classic key agreement mechanisms is only recommended until the end of 2031 (see [TR-02102-1], Section 2.1).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Irrevocably delete ephemeral keys after use and ensure no persistent copies are stored.
evidence_quote: If an SSH connection is secured by means of an encryption algorithm, it must be ensured that all ephemeral keys are deleted irrevocably after they have been used and that no copies of these keys were made.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Validate that random number generators used for keys and signatures belong to approved classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3, or NTG.1.
evidence_quote: A random number generator from one of the classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3 or NTG.1 according to [AIS 20/31] is recommended

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Ensure SSH implementations support only recommended algorithms with high priority and disable weak or obsolete algorithms.
evidence_quote: The application should support the algorithms recommended in this document and the algorithms given in the specification, but be configured in such a way that the recommended (cryptographically strong) algorithms are used with high priority and the (possibly cryptographically weak or obsolete) algorithms from the SSH specification are used with low priority or, if possible, are not used.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Prohibit the use of SSH-1 due to cryptographic vulnerabilities and mandate SSH-2.
evidence_quote: The use of SSH-2 is recommended. Using SSH-1 is not recommended, since this protocol version contains cryptographic vulnerabilities.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Maintain an inventory of supported key exchange methods and their expiration dates to ensure compliance with security levels.
evidence_quote: The following key exchange methods are recommended: ... Use up to 2031

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Catalog encryption algorithms and their validity periods to manage cryptographic estate lifecycle.
evidence_quote: The following encryption algorithms are recommended in this respect: ... Use up to 2032+

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Record signature algorithms for server authentication and their discontinuation dates.
evidence_quote: The following signature algorithms are recommended for server authentication: ... Use up to 2029 / 2032+

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Adopt a security level of 120 bits for all cryptographic mechanisms as defined in organizational policy.
evidence_quote: The security level for all cryptographic mechanisms in this Technical Guideline depends on the security level stated in Section 1.1 in [TR-02102-1] and is 120 bits.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Establish policy requiring secure storage of private keys using certified hardware such as HSMs or chip cards.
evidence_quote: Private cryptographic keys, especially static keys and signature keys, must be stored and processed in a secure manner. ... Secure storage of the keys can be achieved, for example by using certified hardware (chip card, HSM).

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Designate a Chief Information Security Officer responsible for overseeing and implementing the cybersecurity program.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall designate a CISO. The CISO may be employed by the covered entity, one of its affiliates or a third-party service provider.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement and maintain a written cybersecurity policy approved at least annually by senior officers or the governing body.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall implement and maintain a written policy or policies, approved at least annually by a senior officer or the covered entity's senior governing body for the protection of its information systems and nonpublic information stored on those information systems.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Ensure the senior governing body exercises oversight of cybersecurity risk management and confirms resource allocation.
evidence_quote: The senior governing body of the covered entity shall exercise oversight of the covered entity's cybersecurity risk management... confirming that the covered entity's management has allocated sufficient resources to implement and maintain an effective cybersecurity program.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Require the CISO to report in writing at least annually to the senior governing body on program effectiveness and risks.
evidence_quote: The CISO of each covered entity shall report in writing at least annually to the senior governing body on the covered entity's cybersecurity program, including... overall effectiveness of the covered entity's cybersecurity program; and material cybersecurity risks to the covered entity.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Submit an annual certification of material compliance signed by the highest-ranking executive and the CISO.
evidence_quote: Annually each covered entity shall submit to the superintendent electronically... a written certification that certifies that the covered entity materially complied with the requirements set forth in this Part during the prior calendar year... signed by the covered entity's highest-ranking executive and its CISO.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Maintain records supporting the annual compliance certification for a period of five years.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall maintain for examination and inspection by the department upon request all records, schedules and other documentation and data supporting the certification or acknowledgment for a period of five years.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies for encryption of nonpublic information in transit and at rest, or approved compensating controls.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall implement a written policy requiring encryption that meets industry standards, to protect nonpublic information held or transmitted by the covered entity both in transit over external networks and at rest.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for asset inventory management including tracking ownership and classification.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall implement written policies and procedures designed to produce and maintain a complete, accurate and documented asset inventory of the covered entity's information systems... track key information for each asset, including... owner; location; classification or sensitivity.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for the secure disposal of nonpublic information no longer necessary for business operations.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall include policies and procedures for the secure disposal on a periodic basis of any nonpublic information... that is no longer necessary for business operations or for other legitimate business purposes.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for third-party service provider security including due diligence and risk assessment.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall implement written policies and procedures designed to ensure the security of information systems and nonpublic information that are accessible to, or held by, third-party service providers... address... the identification and risk assessment of third-party service providers; minimum cybersecurity practices required...

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for vulnerability management including penetration testing and remediation.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall... develop and implement written policies and procedures for vulnerability management that are designed to assess and maintain the effectiveness of its cybersecurity program... conduct, at a minimum: penetration testing... automated scans... timely remediate vulnerabilities.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for access privileges management including periodic review of user access.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall... limit user access privileges to information systems that provide access to nonpublic information to only those necessary to perform the user's job... periodically, but at a minimum annually, review all user access privileges and remove or disable accounts and access that are no longer necessary.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for incident response and business continuity management including testing.
evidence_quote: Each covered entity shall establish written plans that contain proactive measures to investigate and mitigate cybersecurity events... including but not limited to incident response, business continuity and disaster recovery plans... periodically, but at a minimum annually, test its incident response and BCDR plans.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for application security covering secure development practices.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for risk assessment to inform the design of the cybersecurity program.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for monitoring authorized user activity to detect unauthorized access.

source_name: NY DFS 23 NYCRR Part 500 — Cybersecurity Requirements (Amendment 2)
requirement: Implement written policies and procedures for multi-factor authentication for remote access and privileged accounts.

requirement: Maintain records of audit trails for not fewer than three years.

evidence_quote: For this reason, it is RECOMMENDED that implementations retain and export the seed, even when also exporting the expanded private key.

source_name: Internet X.509 PKI — Algorithm Identifiers for ML-KEM
requirement: Use the seed format for ML-KEM private keys as the recommended representation to ensure compact storage and efficient key management.
evidence_quote: The seed format is RECOMMENDED , as it efficiently stores both the private and public key.

source_name: Internet X.509 PKI — Algorithm Identifiers for ML-KEM
requirement: Include the public key and expanded private key in the seed-only format to enable key pair consistency checks during import operations.
evidence_quote: While the publicKey field and expandedKey format are technically redundant when using the seed-only format, they MAY be included to enable key pair consistency checks during import operations.

source_name: Internet X.509 PKI — Algorithm Identifiers for ML-KEM
requirement: Parse ML-KEM private keys using explicit ASN.1 context-specific tags rather than heuristics like length to correctly identify key variants.
evidence_quote: Implementations should use the context-specific tag IMPLICIT [0] (raw value 0x80 ) for seed , OCTET STRING ( 0x04 ) for expandedKey , and SEQUENCE ( 0x30 ) for both to parse the private key, rather than any other heuristic like length of the enclosing OCTET STRING .

source_name: PQXDH: The Post-Quantum Extended Diffie-Hellman Protocol
requirement: Delete previous private keys for signed prekeys after a retention period to ensure forward secrecy.
evidence_quote: Eventually, Bob should delete this private key for forward secrecy (one-time prekey private keys will be deleted as Bob receives messages using them; see Section 3.4 ).

source_name: PQXDH: The Post-Quantum Extended Diffie-Hellman Protocol
requirement: Delete ephemeral private keys and shared secrets immediately after calculating the session key.
evidence_quote: After calculating SK , Alice deletes her ephemeral private key, the DH outputs and the shared secret SS .

source_name: PQXDH: The Post-Quantum Extended Diffie-Hellman Protocol
requirement: Rotate signed curve and last-resort PQKEM prekeys periodically and replace previous values on the server.
evidence_quote: For both the signed curve prekey and the signed last-resort pqkem prekey, Bob will upload a new prekey along with its signature using IK B at some interval... The new signed prekey and its signatures will replace the previous values.

source_name: PQXDH: The Post-Quantum Extended Diffie-Hellman Protocol
requirement: Delete one-time prekeys from the server immediately after they are used in a protocol run.
evidence_quote: The server should provide one of Bob's curve one-time prekeys if one exists and then delete it.

source_name: PQXDH: The Post-Quantum Extended Diffie-Hellman Protocol
requirement: Define and document specific parameter sets including curves, hash functions, and PQKEM algorithms for the application.
evidence_quote: An application using PQXDH must decide on several parameters: Name Definition curve... hash... info... pqkem... aead...

source_name: PQXDH: The Post-Quantum Extended Diffie-Hellman Protocol
requirement: Implement logic to distinguish between one-time and last-resort PQKEM keys to manage key usage policies.
evidence_quote: An implementation should provide Bob a way to identify whether a pqkem public key corresponds to a one-time pqkem key or a last-resort pqkem key.

source_name: PQXDH: The Post-Quantum Extended Diffie-Hellman Protocol
requirement: Verify signatures on all prekeys before protocol execution and abort if validation fails.
evidence_quote: Alice verifies the signatures on the prekeys. If any signature check fails, Alice aborts the protocol.
