evidence_quote: SP 800-140B also specifies the content of the tabular and graphical information required in ISO/IEC 19790 Annex B. As a validation authority, the Cryptographic Module Validation Program (CMVP) may... specify the order of the security policy as specified in ISO/IEC 19790:2012 B.1.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140B: CMVP Security Policy Requirements
requirement: Modify, add, or delete Vendor Evidence and Test Evidence to align with validation authority requirements for cryptographic modules.
evidence_quote: As a validation authority, the Cryptographic Module Validation Program (CMVP) may modify, add, or delete Vendor Evidence (VE) and/or Test Evidence (TE) specified under paragraph 6.14 of the ISO/IEC 24759

source_name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — Regulation EU 2016/679
requirement: Establish documented policies defining lawful bases, purposes, and retention limits for personal data processing.
evidence_quote: The specific purposes for which personal data are processed should be explicit and legitimate and determined at the time of the collection of the personal data. ... time limits should be established by the controller for erasure or for a periodic review.

source_name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — Regulation EU 2016/679
requirement: Implement technical and organizational measures to ensure pseudonymization and separate storage of attribution data.
evidence_quote: measures of pseudonymisation should, whilst allowing general analysis, be possible within the same controller when that controller has taken technical and organisational measures necessary to ensure, for the processing concerned, that this Regulation is implemented, and that additional information for attributing the personal data to a specific data subject is kept separately.

source_name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — Regulation EU 2016/679
requirement: Define and document the identity of the controller and the purposes of processing to ensure transparency.
evidence_quote: That principle concerns, in particular, information to the data subjects on the identity of the controller and the purposes of the processing and further information to ensure fair and transparent processing in respect of the natural persons concerned

source_name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — Regulation EU 2016/679
requirement: Maintain records demonstrating that data subjects have given valid consent for processing operations.
evidence_quote: Where processing is based on the data subject's consent, the controller should be able to demonstrate that the data subject has given consent to the processing operation.

source_name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — Regulation EU 2016/679
requirement: Ensure processing activities comply with applicable data protection rules and are subject to monitoring by supervisory authorities.
evidence_quote: The processing of personal data by those public authorities should comply with the applicable data-protection rules according to the purposes of the processing.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Designate a supervisory body to carry out supervisory activities under the Regulation.
evidence_quote: Member States should designate a supervisory body or supervisory bodies to carry out the supervisory activities under this Regulation.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Apply good security practice appropriate to the risks related to trust service activities.
evidence_quote: It should be incumbent on all trust service providers to apply good security practice appropriate to the risks related to their activities so as to boost users' trust in the single market.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Ensure due diligence, transparency, and accountability of operations and services.
evidence_quote: All trust service providers should be subject to the requirements of this Regulation, in particular those on security and liability to ensure due diligence, transparency and accountability of their operations and services.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Establish liability for damage caused by failure to comply with regulatory obligations.
evidence_quote: This Regulation should provide for the liability of all trust service providers. In particular, it establishes the liability regime under which all trust service providers should be liable for damage caused to any natural or legal person due to failure to comply with the obligations under this Regulation.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Implement termination plans to ensure sustainability and durability of qualified trust services.
evidence_quote: To ensure sustainability and durability of qualified trust services and to boost users' confidence in the continuity of qualified trust services, supervisory bodies should verify the existence and the correct application of provisions on termination plans in cases where qualified trust service providers cease their activities.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Submit conformity assessment reports to the supervisory body to ensure compliance.
evidence_quote: In order to ensure the compliance of qualified trust service providers and the services they provide with the requirements set out in this Regulation, a conformity assessment should be carried out by a conformity assessment body and the resulting conformity assessment reports should be submitted by the qualified trust service providers to the supervisory body.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Report security incidents and personal data breaches to supervisory bodies and data protection authorities.
evidence_quote: Supervisory bodies should cooperate with data protection authorities, for example, by informing them about the results of audits of qualified trust service providers, where personal data protection rules appear to have been breached. The provision of information should in particular cover security incidents and personal data breaches.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Notify security breaches and conduct security risk assessments to provide adequate information.
evidence_quote: Notification of security breaches and security risk assessments is essential with a view to providing adequate information to concerned parties in the event of a breach of security or loss of integrity.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Report supervisory activities to the Commission and ENISA to assess effectiveness.
evidence_quote: To enable the Commission and the Member States to assess the effectiveness of the enhanced supervision mechanism introduced by this Regulation, supervisory bodies should be requested to report on their activities.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Ensure long-term preservation of information to guarantee legal validity over extended periods.
evidence_quote: This Regulation should ensure the long-term preservation of information, in order to ensure the legal validity of electronic signatures and electronic seals over extended periods of time and guarantee that they can be validated irrespective of future technological changes.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Clearly indicate the suspension status and precise period of time for suspended certificates.
evidence_quote: Legal certainty calls for the suspension status of a certificate to always be clearly indicated. To that end, trust service providers should have the responsibility to clearly indicate the status of the certificate and, if suspended, the precise period of time during which the certificate has been suspended.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Utilize only CMVP-approved methods for sensitive security parameter generation and establishment, precluding all other methods.
evidence_quote: This document identifies CMVP-approved sensitive security parameter generation and establishment methods. It precludes the use of all other sensitive security parameter generation and establishment methods.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Adhere to NIST SP 800-131A Rev. 2 for transitioning cryptographic algorithms and key lengths in parameter generation.
evidence_quote: Barker EB, Roginsky AL (2019) Transitioning the Use of Cryptographic Algorithms and Key Lengths. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-131A, Rev. 2.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Implement key establishment techniques for DSA, RSA, and ECDSA strictly according to FIPS 186-4.
evidence_quote: National Institute of Standards and Technology (2013) Digital Signature Standard (DSS). (U.S. Department of Commerce, Washington, DC), Federal Information Processing Standards Publication (FIPS) 186-4. DSA, RSA, and ECDSA.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Execute pair-wise key establishment using discrete logarithm cryptography per NIST SP 800-56A Rev. 3.
evidence_quote: Barker EB, Chen L, Roginsky AL, Vassilev A, Davis R (2018) Recommendation for Pair-Wise Key-Establishment Schemes Using Discrete Logarithm Cryptography. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-56A, Rev. 3.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Execute pair-wise key establishment using integer factorization cryptography per NIST SP 800-56B Rev. 2.
evidence_quote: Barker EB, Chen L, Roginsky AL, Vassilev A, Davis R, Simon S (2019) Recommendation for Pair-Wise Key-Establishment Using Integer Factorization Cryptography. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-56B, Rev. 2.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Derive keys using pseudorandom functions in accordance with NIST SP 800-108 Revised.
evidence_quote: Chen L (2009) Recommendation for Key Derivation Using Pseudorandom Functions (Revised). (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-108, Revised.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Derive keys from passwords for storage applications following NIST SP 800-132.
evidence_quote: Sönmez Turan M, Barker EB, Burr WE, Chen L (2010) Recommendation for Password-Based Key Derivation: Part 1: Storage Applications. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-132.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Utilize existing application-specific key derivation functions as defined in NIST SP 800-135 Rev. 1.
evidence_quote: Dang QH (2011) Recommendation for Existing Application-Specific Key Derivation Functions. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-135, Rev. 1.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Apply key-derivation methods in key-establishment schemes per NIST SP 800-56C Rev. 1.
evidence_quote: Barker EB, Chen L, Davis R (2018) Recommendation for Key-Derivation Methods in Key-Establishment Schemes. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-56C, Rev. 1.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Perform key derivation through extraction-then-expansion according to NIST SP 800-56C.
evidence_quote: Chen L (2011) Recommendation for Key-Derivation through Extraction-then-Expansion. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-56C.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Wrap keys using block cipher modes of operation as specified in NIST SP 800-38F.
evidence_quote: Dworkin MJ (2012) Recommendation for Block Cipher Modes of Operation: Methods for Key Wrapping. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-38F.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Generate cryptographic keys strictly following NIST SP 800-133 Rev. 1.
evidence_quote: Barker EB, Roginsky AL (2019) Recommendation for Cryptographic Key Generation. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-133, Rev. 1.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Implement deterministic random bit generators for random number generation per NIST SP 800-90A Rev. 1.
evidence_quote: Barker EB, Kelsey J (2015) Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-90A, Rev. 1.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Validate entropy sources used for random number generation in accordance with NIST SP 800-90B.
evidence_quote: Sonmez Turan M, Barker EB, Kelsey J, McKay KA, Baish, ML, Boyle M (2018) Recommendation for Entropy Sources Used for Random Number Generation. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-90B.

source_name: EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) — Regulation EU 2024/2847
requirement: Establish documented policies and processes for cybersecurity risk management throughout the product lifecycle.
evidence_quote: manufacturers take security seriously throughout a product’s lifecycle... Manufacturers should therefore ensure that all products with digital elements are designed and developed in accordance with the essential cybersecurity requirements laid down in this Regulation.

source_name: EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) — Regulation EU 2024/2847
requirement: Ensure staff possesses necessary cybersecurity skills to comply with regulatory obligations.
evidence_quote: manufacturers should ensure that their staff has the necessary skills to comply with their obligations as laid down in this Regulation.

source_name: EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) — Regulation EU 2024/2847
requirement: Generate and maintain Software Bills of Materials (SBOMs) to document software components and dependencies.
evidence_quote: Market surveillance authorities should be able to request manufacturers of categories of products with digital elements established by ADCO to submit the software bills of materials (SBOMs) that they have generated pursuant to this Regulation.

source_name: EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) — Regulation EU 2024/2847
requirement: Implement processes to provide security updates and manage vulnerabilities throughout the product support period.
evidence_quote: insufficient and inconsistent provision of security updates to address them... ensuring that hardware and software products are placed on the market with fewer vulnerabilities and that manufacturers take security seriously throughout a product’s lifecycle.

source_name: EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) — Regulation EU 2024/2847
requirement: Undergo conformity assessment procedures to verify compliance with essential cybersecurity requirements before market placement.
evidence_quote: Consumer products with digital elements categorised in this Regulation as important products with digital elements... should undergo a stricter conformity assessment procedure.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140E — CMVP Approved Authentication Mechanisms
requirement: Ensure authentication mechanisms for cryptographic modules meet CMVP-approved requirements and align with SP 800-63B standards.
evidence_quote: This document includes all requirements for CMVP-approved authentication mechanisms for operators. Vendors should use SP 800-63B as a framework for authentication requirements and should provide justification whenever SP 800-63B requirements cannot be met.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140E — CMVP Approved Authentication Mechanisms
requirement: Validate that operator authentication is performed by the module or Operating Environment for FIPS 140-3 levels above Level 1.
evidence_quote: Except at level 1, operator authentication acceptance is required to be performed by the module or by the Operating Environment as defined in ISO/IEC 19790.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140E — CMVP Approved Authentication Mechanisms
requirement: Confirm that authentication mechanisms used at FIPS 140-3 Level 2 or higher meet the specified approved mechanisms in Table 1.
evidence_quote: While there are currently no approved authentication mechanisms, allowed authentication mechanisms may be used as indicated in Table 1.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140E — CMVP Approved Authentication Mechanisms
requirement: Establish documented justification when SP 800-63B authentication requirements cannot be met for cryptographic modules.
evidence_quote: Vendors should use SP 800-63B as a framework for authentication requirements and should provide justification whenever SP 800-63B requirements cannot be met.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140E — CMVP Approved Authentication Mechanisms
requirement: Incorporate lifecycle management and session management practices from SP 800-63B for authenticators in cryptographic modules.
evidence_quote: Normative SP 800-63B sections include Section 5, Detailed requirements specific to each type of authenticator; Section 6, Lifecycle management; Section 7, Session Management.

source_name: Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) — Regulation EU 2023/1114
requirement: Establish effective administrative arrangements to ensure systems and security protocols meet Union standards.
evidence_quote: Offerors and persons seeking admission to trading of crypto-assets other than asset-referenced tokens or e-money tokens should... have effective administrative arrangements to ensure that their systems and security protocols meet Union standards.

source_name: Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) — Regulation EU 2023/1114
requirement: Implement arrangements to identify, prevent, manage, and disclose conflicts of interest.
evidence_quote: Offerors and persons seeking admission to trading of crypto-assets other than asset-referenced tokens or e-money tokens should... identify, prevent, manage and disclose conflicts of interest

source_name: Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) — Regulation EU 2023/1114
requirement: Ensure funds or crypto-assets raised during public offers are safeguarded by a third party.
evidence_quote: The offeror should ensure that the funds or other crypto-assets collected during the offer to the public are safeguarded by a third party.

source_name: Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) — Regulation EU 2023/1114
requirement: Maintain effective arrangements to monitor and safeguard funds or crypto-assets raised during public offers.
evidence_quote: Offerors of crypto-assets other than asset-referenced tokens or e-money tokens should have effective arrangements in place to monitor and safeguard the funds or other crypto-assets raised during their offer to the public.

source_name: Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) — Regulation EU 2023/1114
requirement: Draw up and publish a crypto-asset white paper containing mandatory disclosures on risks, technology, and issuer details.
evidence_quote: offerors or persons seeking admission to trading should draw up, notify to their competent authority and publish an information document containing mandatory disclosures ('a crypto-asset white paper').

source_name: Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) — Regulation EU 2023/1114
requirement: Notify the crypto-asset white paper and marketing communications to the competent authority before public offer or admission to trading.
evidence_quote: offerors and persons seeking admission to trading of crypto-assets other than asset-referenced tokens or e-money tokens should, before making any offer to the public... notify their crypto-asset white paper and, upon request of the competent authority, their marketing communications, to the competent authority

source_name: Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) — Regulation EU 2023/1114
requirement: Ensure advertising messages and marketing material are consistent with the information provided in the crypto-asset white paper.
evidence_quote: Advertising messages and marketing material should be consistent with the information provided in the crypto-asset white paper.

source_name: Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) — Regulation EU 2023/1114
requirement: Act honestly, fairly, and professionally when communicating with holders and prospective holders of crypto-assets.
evidence_quote: Offerors and persons seeking admission to trading of crypto-assets other than asset-referenced tokens or e-money tokens should act honestly, fairly and professionally, should communicate with holders and prospective holders of crypto-assets in a manner that is fair, clear and not misleading

source_name: EU Commission Recommendation on PQC — 2024/1101
requirement: Develop a comprehensive strategy defining goals, milestones, and timelines for Post-Quantum Cryptography adoption.
evidence_quote: This Commission Recommendation encourages Member States to develop a comprehensive strategy for the adoption of Post-Quantum Cryptography... The strategy should define clear goals, milestones, and timelines resulting in the definition of a joint Post-Quantum Cryptography Implementation Roadmap.

source_name: EU Commission Recommendation on PQC — 2024/1101
requirement: Establish a dedicated sub-group with expert representatives to coordinate the development of the PQC Implementation Roadmap.
evidence_quote: Member States are invited to establish such a sub-group on Post-Quantum Cryptography... and to appoint expert representatives who should work in close cooperation with the Commission and who should be tasked to define and develop the Post-Quantum Cryptography Coordinated Implementation Roadmap.

source_name: EU Commission Recommendation on PQC — 2024/1101
requirement: Align national transition plans with the coordinated EU Post-Quantum Cryptography Implementation Roadmap.
evidence_quote: Once agreed by the Member States, the Post-Quantum Cryptography Coordinated Implementation Roadmap should serve as blueprint for the definition of the national transition plans towards Post Quantum Cryptography, or, where national plans exist, their alignment with the common Post-Quantum Cryptography Coordinated Implementation Roadmap.

source_name: EU Commission Recommendation on PQC — 2024/1101
requirement: Submit relevant information to the Commission upon request to enable monitoring of PQC transition progress.
evidence_quote: Member States are therefore encouraged to submit to the Commission, upon its request, all relevant information, which they can reasonably be expected to provide, to ensure such monitoring.

source_name: EU Commission Recommendation on PQC — 2024/1101
requirement: Cooperate with the Commission to assess the effects of the Recommendation within three years of publication.
evidence_quote: Member States should cooperate with the Commission to assess the effects of this Recommendation maximum three years after its publication, with a view to determine appropriate ways forward.

source_name: NIST Automated Cryptographic Validation Protocol (ACVP)
requirement: Validate Random Number Generation implementations against DRBG requirements through the CAVP Automated Cryptographic Validation Protocol.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Identify a security official responsible for developing and implementing security policies and procedures.
evidence_quote: Standard: Assigned security responsibility. Identify the security official who is responsible for the development and implementation of the policies and procedures required by this subpart for the covered entity or business associate.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations.
evidence_quote: Standard: Security management process. Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Apply appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply with security policies and procedures.
evidence_quote: Sanction policy (Required). Apply appropriate sanctions against workforce members who fail to comply with the security policies and procedures of the covered entity or business associate.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to electronic protected health information.
evidence_quote: Standard: Information access management. Implement policies and procedures for authorizing access to electronic protected health information that are consistent with the applicable requirements of subpart E of this part.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents.
evidence_quote: Standard: Security incident procedures. Implement policies and procedures to address security incidents.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Establish policies and procedures for responding to emergencies that damage systems containing electronic protected health information.
evidence_quote: Standard: Contingency plan. Establish (and implement as needed) policies and procedures for responding to an emergency or other occurrence (for example, fire, vandalism, system failure, and natural disaster) that damages systems that contain electronic protected health information.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to electronic information systems and facilities.
evidence_quote: Standard: Facility access controls. Implement policies and procedures to limit physical access to its electronic information systems and the facility or facilities in which they are housed, while ensuring that properly authorized access is allowed.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement policies and procedures governing the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media containing electronic protected health information.
evidence_quote: Standard: Device and media controls. Implement policies and procedures that govern the receipt and removal of hardware and electronic media that contain electronic protected health information into and out of a facility, and the movement of these items within the facility.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems to allow access only to authorized persons or software programs.
evidence_quote: Standard: Access control. Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain electronic protected health information to allow access only to those persons or software programs that have been granted access rights as specified in § 164.308(a)(4).

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement policies and procedures to protect electronic protected health information from improper alteration or destruction.
evidence_quote: Standard: Integrity. Implement policies and procedures to protect electronic protected health information from improper alteration or destruction.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to electronic protected health information is the one claimed.
evidence_quote: Standard: Person or entity authentication. Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to electronic protected health information is the one claimed.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to electronic protected health information being transmitted over an electronic communications network.
evidence_quote: Standard: Transmission security. Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to electronic protected health information that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement reasonable and appropriate policies and procedures to comply with the standards, implementation specifications, or other requirements of this subpart.
evidence_quote: Standard: Policies and procedures. Implement reasonable and appropriate policies and procedures to comply with the standards, implementation specifications, or other requirements of this subpart, taking into account those factors specified in § 164.306(b)(2)(i), (ii), (iii), and (iv).

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of electronic protected health information.
evidence_quote: Risk analysis (Required). Conduct an accurate and thorough assessment of the potential risks and vulnerabilities to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of electronic protected health information held by the covered entity or business associate.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.
evidence_quote: Information system activity review (Required). Implement procedures to regularly review records of information system activity, such as audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking reports.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Identify and respond to suspected or known security incidents; mitigate harmful effects; and document security incidents and their outcomes.
evidence_quote: Response and reporting (Required). Identify and respond to suspected or known security incidents; mitigate, to the extent practicable, harmful effects of security incidents that are known to the covered entity or business associate; and document security incidents and their outcomes.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Perform a periodic technical and nontechnical evaluation to establish the extent to which security policies and procedures meet the requirements of this subpart.
evidence_quote: Standard: Evaluation. Perform a periodic technical and nontechnical evaluation, based initially upon the standards implemented under this rule and, subsequently, in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of electronic protected health information, that establishes the extent to which a covered entity's or business associate's security policies and procedures meet…

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.
evidence_quote: Standard: Audit controls. Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Maintain written or electronic records of policies and procedures implemented to comply with this subpart.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Retain documentation required by this subpart for 6 years from the date of its creation or the date when it last was in effect, whichever is later.
evidence_quote: Time limit (Required). Retain the documentation required by paragraph (b)(1) of this section for 6 years from the date of its creation or the date when it last was in effect, whichever is later.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement procedures for terminating access to electronic protected health information when employment or other arrangements with a workforce member end.
evidence_quote: Termination procedures (Addressable). source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement policies and procedures to address the final disposition of electronic protected health information and/or the hardware or electronic media on which it is stored.
evidence_quote: Disposal (Required). Implement policies and procedures to address the final disposition of electronic protected health information, and/or the hardware or electronic media on which it is stored.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Implement procedures for removal of electronic protected health information from electronic media before the media are made available for re-use.
evidence_quote: Media re-use (Required). Implement procedures for removal of electronic protected health information from electronic media before the media are made available for re-use.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of electronic protected health information.
evidence_quote: Data backup plan (Required). Establish and implement procedures to create and maintain retrievable exact copies of electronic protected health information.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Establish and implement procedures to restore any loss of data.
evidence_quote: Disaster recovery plan (Required). Establish (and implement as needed) procedures to restore any loss of data.

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164

source_name: HIPAA Security Rule — 45 CFR Part 164
requirement: Review documentation periodically and update as needed in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of electronic protected health information.
evidence_quote: Updates (Required). Review documentation periodically, and update as needed, in response to environmental or operational changes affecting the security of the electronic protected health information.

evidence_quote: La confidentialité et l'intégrité de la clé pré-partagée sont des conditions préalables essentielles. Chaque clé pré-partagée ne doit être partagée que par deux parties et non par un groupe de trois parties ou plus.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Use well-studied standards or modes with validated security proofs for cryptographic functions and hybridation.
evidence_quote: l'ANSSI recommande d'utiliser des normes ou des modes bien étudiés avec des preuves de sécurité validées.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Require hybridation implementation for final products with post-quantum protection, unless using specific hash-based signatures.
evidence_quote: tout produit qui inclut une protection post-quantique doit mettre en œuvre l'hybridation, sauf dans les rares cas où la protection post-quantique repose uniquement sur des signatures basées sur le hachage comme XMSS, LMS ou SPHINCS+, pour lesquelles l'hybridation est facultative

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Include user guides recommending exclusive use of post-quantum algorithms in combination with classical algorithms for platform products.
evidence_quote: l'inclusion dans les guides pour les utilisateurs d'une recommandation d'utiliser exclusivement l'algorithme post-quantique fourni en combinaison avec un algorithme classique reconnu dans le cadre d'un mode d'hybridation.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Enforce TLS 1.3 as the preferred protocol version and disable TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 immediately.
evidence_quote: TLS 1.3 should be used in preference. TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are not recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Retire TLS 1.2 support by the end of 2031 due to lack of quantum-safe key agreement mechanisms.
evidence_quote: TLS 1.2 is only recommended until the end of 2031, since, based on current knowledge, no quantum-safe key agreement mechanisms will be standardized for TLS 1.2.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Discontinue DSA and DHE cipher suite recommendations by the end of 2029.
evidence_quote: Adjustment of the periods of use, discontinuation of DSA and of DHE cipher suite recommendations from the end of 2029

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Migrate away from RSA signatures with PKCS #1 v1.5 padding by the end of 2025.
evidence_quote: The use of the signature algorithm rsa (IANA no. 1) is recommended only up to 2025, because it uses the PKCS #1 v1.5 padding scheme.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Disable the Heartbeat extension to prevent memory disclosure vulnerabilities.
evidence_quote: It is recommended not to use the Heartbeat extension.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Implement the Encrypt-then-MAC extension for all CBC-mode cipher suites to prevent padding oracle attacks.
evidence_quote: The use of the TLS extension 'encrypt-then-MAC' according to [RFC 7366] is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Implement the Extended Master Secret extension to mitigate triple handshake attacks.
evidence_quote: Using the TLS extension Extended Master Secret according to [RFC 7627] is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Disable TLS data compression to prevent CRIME side-channel attacks.
evidence_quote: If this cannot be ensured, it is recommended not to use TLS data compression.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Reject client-initiated session renegotiation to prevent downgrade attacks.
evidence_quote: Renegotiation initiated by the client should by rejected by the server.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Avoid using 0-RTT data in TLS 1.3 PSK handshakes due to lack of replay protection.
evidence_quote: Therefore, sending or accepting 0-RTT data is not recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Ensure ephemeral keys are deleted irrevocably after use and never stored persistently.
evidence_quote: It must be ensured that all ephemeral keys are deleted irrevocably after they have been used and that no copies of these keys were made.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Validate random number generators against BSI AIS 20/31 classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3, or NTG.1.
evidence_quote: A random number generator from one of the classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3 or NTG.1 according to [AIS 20/31] is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Inventory and restrict TLS cipher suites to those listed in the BSI recommendations with Perfect Forward Secrecy.
evidence_quote: The use of the following cipher suites with Perfect Forward Secrecy is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Inventory and restrict Diffie-Hellman groups to approved named curves to avoid weak domain parameters.
evidence_quote: It is recommended to only use named curves... in order to avoid attacks via unverified weak domain parameters.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Plan migration to quantum-safe hybrid key agreement mechanisms by the end of 2031.
evidence_quote: The sole use of classic key agreement mechanisms is only recommended until the end of 2031.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Define security policies specifying SA lifetimes and data volume limits for renegotiation.
evidence_quote: Indicating binding periods of validity or an upper limit for the data volume is part of a security policy and must be defined by the system administrator.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Define security requirements for SA lifetimes based on application needs.
evidence_quote: The SA lifetime should be defined depending on the security requirements of the application.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Renegotiate IKE-SAs and IPsec-SAs upon expiration of defined time or data volume limits.
evidence_quote: Both an IKE-SA and an IPsec-SA should only be valid for a limited period of time and renegotiated after this period of time has expired.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Limit IKE-SA lifetime to 24 hours and IPsec-SA lifetime to 4 hours in standard scenarios.
evidence_quote: In ordinary operating scenarios, the IKE-SA lifetime should not exceed 24 h and the IPsec-SA lifetime should not exceed 4 h.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Delete nonces and long-term keys immediately after single use in signature generation.
evidence_quote: The nonce as well as the long-term key must be kept secret and deleted immediately after they have been used once.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Use random number generators from approved classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3, or NTG.1.
evidence_quote: A random number generator from one of the classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3 or NTG.1 according to [AIS 20/31] is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Store private cryptographic keys in certified hardware such as chip cards or HSMs.
evidence_quote: Secure storage of the keys can be achieved, for example by using certified hardware (chip card, HSM).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Protect Security Association Database and Security Policy Database from manipulation.
evidence_quote: The SAD and SPD databases must be stored in a secure manner in order to prevent them from being manipulated by attackers.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Identify and mitigate side-channel and fault attack vulnerabilities through expert involvement.
evidence_quote: The developer should identify possible side channels by involving experts in this field and implement corresponding countermeasures.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Migrate from sole classical key agreement mechanisms to hybrid quantum-safe mechanisms by end of 2031.
evidence_quote: The sole use of classic key agreement mechanisms is only recommended until the end of 2031.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Establish documented policy mandating SSH-2 usage and prohibiting SSH-1 due to cryptographic vulnerabilities.
evidence_quote: The use of SSH-2 is recommended. Using SSH-1 is not recommended, since this protocol version contains cryptographic vulnerabilities.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Define policy to prioritize recommended strong algorithms over weak or obsolete algorithms specified in SSH standards.
evidence_quote: The application should support the algorithms recommended in this document and the algorithms given in the specification, but be configured in such a way that the recommended (cryptographically strong) algorithms are used with high priority and the (possibly cryptographically weak or obsolete) algorithms from the SSH specification are used with low priority or, if possible, are not used.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Mandate secure storage of private keys using certified hardware to prevent copying, misuse, and manipulation.
evidence_quote: Private cryptographic keys, especially static keys and signature keys, must be stored and processed in a secure manner. This includes, among other things, the protection against copying, misuse and manipulation of the keys. Secure storage of the keys can be achieved, for example by using certified hardware (chip card, HSM).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Enforce policy requiring irrevocable deletion of ephemeral keys after use and prohibit persistent storage.
evidence_quote: It must be ensured that all ephemeral keys are deleted irrevocably after they have been used and that no copies of these keys were made. Ephemeral or session keys may only be used for one connection and generally not be stored persistently.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Require use of random number generators from approved classes (DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3, or NTG.1) for key generation.
evidence_quote: A random number generator from one of the classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3 or NTG.1 according to [AIS 20/31] is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Implement key re-exchange policy to renew session keys after one hour or one gigabyte of data transmitted.
evidence_quote: It is recommended to re-exchange the keys according to Chapter 9 in [RFC 4253], i.e. the session keys are renewed after one hour or after one gigabyte has been transmitted (whichever happens first).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Plan migration to quantum-safe mechanisms by end of 2031 as sole use of classical key agreement is no longer recommended.
evidence_quote: The sole use of classic key agreement mechanisms is only recommended until the end of 2031.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Discontinue use of DSA signature algorithms by end of 2029.
evidence_quote: The use of the signature algorithm pgp-sign-dss is recommended only up to 2029, because it is not widely employed and no longer approved in [FIPS 186-5].

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for SSH
requirement: Maintain inventory of supported key exchange methods and enforce usage limits based on defined end-of-life years.
evidence_quote: The recommendations in this Technical Guideline have a maximum period of use. The indication of the year means that the respective mechanism can be used until the end of the year stated.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Ensure the ITSEF complies with process-specific requirements and provides information on ongoing evaluation procedures to the certification body.
evidence_quote: The ITSEF shall comply with the process-specific requirements pursuant to Section 2... and shall provide information to the product certification body (CB) on the process and content of ongoing evaluation procedures.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Immediately notify the certification body of any delays in scheduled procedures to allow for schedule adjustment.
evidence_quote: The ITSEF will immediately notify the certification body (CB) of any delays in the scheduled procedures.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Ensure the ITSEF is responsible for the technical correctness and truthful documentation of evaluation results.
evidence_quote: The ITSEF is responsible for the technical correctness and the truthful documentation of its evaluation results.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Implement adequate security measures to protect the TOE and confidential information when using test equipment outside ITSEF premises.
evidence_quote: Adequate security measures shall be implemented to protect the TOE, confidential information and the procedure-specific knowledge of the evaluator.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Identify updates or additions to the Evaluation Technical Report using change markers and provide both marked and clean versions.
evidence_quote: Any updates or additions to the ETR shall be identified, e.g. by inserting change markers. The PDF documents delivered shall each contain a variant with visible change markers and a 'clean' version with the change markers removed.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Include names and perceived roles of all individuals involved in the preparation of the Evaluation Technical Report.
evidence_quote: Evaluation Technical Reports (ETR) contain the names and perceived roles of the individuals involved in the preparation of the report and the results presented therein.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Sign the final Evaluation Technical Report with specific wording by the project manager and the Quality Management Officer.
evidence_quote: The final version of the ETR... is signed by name by the project manager responsible for the evaluation project or the ITSEF manager with the specific wording... and by the Quality Management Officer (German: QMB) of the ITSEF with the specific wording.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Encrypt all transmitted documents with the corresponding procedure key when exchanging documents electronically.
evidence_quote: All transmitted documents shall be encrypted with the corresponding procedure key.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Ensure processes within the ITSEF enable adequate information exchange between participants and access to relevant manufacturer information.
evidence_quote: The processes within the ITSEF shall ensure an adequate exchange of information between the participants and implemented in practice, and that the participants are able to access all the manufacturer's information and evaluation results relevant to them.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Enable the certification body to monitor individual evaluation steps on the ITSEF premises where necessary.
evidence_quote: The ITSEF shall enable the certification body (CB) to monitor individual evaluation steps on the premises of the ITSEF where necessary.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Take fundamental technical decisions made by the certification body into account independently in future procedures.
evidence_quote: The ITSEF shall take these decisions into account independently in future procedures.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Observe specific requirements for evaluators working from their home office as defined in the AH-Evaluatoren document.
evidence_quote: If the evaluators are working from their home office, they shall observe the requirements in the document 'AH-Evaluatoren' [AH-Evaluatoren] (HO evaluators).

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Notify the certification body of any substantial findings regarding security risks or deviations that would prevent certification.
evidence_quote: The ITSEF shall notify the certification body (CB) of any substantial findings.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Archive all evidence relevant to the evaluation and the evaluated product for the period of validity of the certificate plus three years.
evidence_quote: Archive all the evidence relevant to the evaluation and relating to the procedure the evaluated product for the period of validity of the certificate plus 3 years

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Archive the evaluation object after completion of the evaluation in accordance with BSI certification body specifications.
evidence_quote: The archiving of the evaluation object after completion of the evaluation is carried out in accordance with the specifications of the BSI certification body (CB) taking into account the manufacturer's statement.

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Assess Impact Analysis Reports for re-evaluations or recertifications to determine evaluation effort.
evidence_quote: For re-evaluations or recertifications: assess the Impact Analysis Report (IAR)

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Conduct evaluations including compliance and penetration tests and document the results in the Evaluation Technical Report.
evidence_quote: Conduct of the evaluation with compliance and penetration tests... Documentation of the results

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Create and submit the Evaluation Technical Report to the certification body and update it following comments.
evidence_quote: Creation of the Evaluation Technical Report (ETR)... Submission of the ETR to the certification body (CB)... Update the ETR following comments from the certification body (CB)

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Sign the approved Evaluation Technical Report and submit it to the certification body.
evidence_quote: Sign the approved ETR and submit it to the certification body (CB)

source_name: BSI BSZ — Accelerated Security Certification Evaluation Process (v2.1)
requirement: Provide brief status reports regarding compliance with the agreed schedule to the applicant.
evidence_quote: The ITSEF may inform the applicant via brief status reports regarding (non-)compliance with the agreed schedule.

source_name: COPPA Rule — 16 CFR Part 312
requirement: Designate one or more employees to coordinate the information security program.
evidence_quote: Designate one or more employees to coordinate the operator's information security program

source_name: COPPA Rule — 16 CFR Part 312
requirement: Establish, implement, and maintain a written information security program with appropriate safeguards.
evidence_quote: At a minimum, the operator must establish, implement, and maintain a written information security program that contains safeguards that are appropriate to the sensitivity of the personal information collected from children

source_name: COPPA Rule — 16 CFR Part 312
requirement: Establish, implement, and maintain a written data retention policy defining purposes, business needs, and deletion timeframes.
evidence_quote: At a minimum, the operator must establish, implement, and maintain a written data retention policy that sets forth the purposes for which children's personal information is collected, the business need for retaining such information, and a timeframe for deletion of such information.

source_name: COPPA Rule — 16 CFR Part 312
requirement: Obtain written assurances from third parties that they will employ reasonable measures to maintain confidentiality, security, and integrity.
evidence_quote: the operator must take reasonable steps to determine that such entities are capable of maintaining the confidentiality, security, and integrity of the information and must obtain written assurances that such entities will employ reasonable measures to maintain the confidentiality, security, and integrity of the information.

source_name: COPPA Rule — 16 CFR Part 312
requirement: Delete personal information using reasonable measures to protect against unauthorized access when no longer reasonably necessary.
evidence_quote: When such information is no longer reasonably necessary for the purposes for which it was collected, the operator must delete the information using reasonable measures to protect against unauthorized access to, or use of, the information in connection with its deletion.

source_name: COPPA Rule — 16 CFR Part 312
requirement: Delete parent identification and images promptly after verification is complete.
evidence_quote: where the parent's identification is deleted by the operator from its records promptly after such verification is complete

source_name: COPPA Rule — 16 CFR Part 312
requirement: Delete audio files containing a child's voice immediately after responding to the request.
evidence_quote: the operator deletes such audio files immediately after responding to the request for which they were collected

source_name: COPPA Rule — 16 CFR Part 312
requirement: Identify internal and external risks to confidentiality, security, and integrity at least annually.
evidence_quote: Identify and, at least annually, perform additional assessments to identify internal and external risks to the confidentiality, security, and integrity of personal information collected from children

source_name: COPPA Rule — 16 CFR Part 312
requirement: Regularly test and monitor the effectiveness of safeguards in place to control identified risks.
evidence_quote: Regularly test and monitor the effectiveness of the safeguards in place to control risks identified through the risk assessments required under paragraph (b)(2) of this section

source_name: COPPA Rule — 16 CFR Part 312
requirement: Evaluate and modify the information security program at least annually to address identified risks and new methods.
evidence_quote: At least annually, evaluate and modify the information security program to address identified risks, results of required testing and monitoring, new or more efficient technological or operational methods to control for identified risks

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Establish a systematic risk-based CSMS defining organizational processes, responsibilities, and governance to treat cyber threats.
evidence_quote: Cyber Security Management System (CSMS) means a systematic risk-based approach defining organisational processes, responsibilities and governance to treat risk associated with cyber threats to vehicles and protect them from cyberattacks.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Submit a signed declaration attesting that necessary CSMS processes are installed and will be maintained.
evidence_quote: Manufacturer Name attests that the necessary processes to comply with the requirements for the Cyber Security Management System laid down in paragraph 7.2 of UN Regulation 155 are installed and will be maintained.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Demonstrate processes to manage cyber security, identify risks, assess and treat risks, and verify risk management.
evidence_quote: The vehicle manufacturer shall demonstrate that the processes used within their Cyber Security Management System ensure security is adequately considered, including risks and mitigations listed in Annex 5. This shall include: The processes used within the manufacturer’s organization to manage cyber security; The processes used for the identification of risks to vehicle types.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Demonstrate processes to manage dependencies with contracted suppliers, service providers, or sub-organizations regarding CSMS requirements.
evidence_quote: The vehicle manufacturer shall be required to demonstrate how their Cyber Security Management System will manage dependencies that may exist with contracted suppliers, service providers or manufacturer’s sub-organizations in regards of the requirements of paragraph 7.2.2.2.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Ensure the CSMS covers the development, production, and post-production phases of the vehicle lifecycle.
evidence_quote: The vehicle manufacturer shall demonstrate to an Approval Authority or Technical Service that their Cyber Security Management System applies to the following phases: (a) Development phase; (b) Production phase; (c) Post-production phase.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Obtain a Certificate of Compliance for CSMS from an Approval Authority to validate the management system.
evidence_quote: Contracting Parties shall appoint an Approval Authority to carry out the assessment of the manufacturer and to issue a Certificate of Compliance for CSMS.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Maintain the Certificate of Compliance for CSMS for a maximum of three years subject to verification.
evidence_quote: The Certificate of Compliance for CSMS shall remain valid for a maximum of three years from the date of deliverance of the certificate unless it is withdrawn.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Allow the Approval Authority to verify at any time that CSMS requirements continue to be met.
evidence_quote: The Approval Authority or its Technical Service may at any time verify that the requirements for it continue to be met.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Report annually to the Approval Authority on monitoring activities, new cyber-attacks, and the effectiveness of mitigations.
evidence_quote: The vehicle manufacturer shall report at least once a year, or more frequently if relevant, to the Approval Authority or the Technical Service the outcome of their monitoring activities... this shall include relevant information on new cyber-attacks.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Retain formal documentation packages and additional material for at least 10 years after production discontinuation.
evidence_quote: The Approval Authority or its Technical Service shall ensure that this documentation package remains available for at least 10 years counted from the time when production of the vehicle type is definitively discontinued.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Record conformity of production test results and retain annexed documents for a period not exceeding 10 years after production discontinuation.
evidence_quote: The holder of the approval shall ensure that results of the conformity of production tests are recorded and that the annexed documents remain available for a period determined in agreement with the Approval Authority or its Technical Service. This period shall not exceed 10 years counted from the time when production is definitively discontinued

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Implement processes to monitor, detect, and respond to cyber-attacks and vulnerabilities throughout the vehicle lifecycle.
evidence_quote: The processes used to monitor for, detect and respond to cyber-attacks, cyber threats and vulnerabilities on vehicle types and the processes used to assess whether the cyber security measures implemented are still effective in the light of new cyber threats and vulnerabilities that have been identified.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Ensure monitoring of vehicles includes those after first registration and respects privacy rights.
evidence_quote: The vehicle manufacturer shall demonstrate that the processes used within their Cyber Security Management System will ensure that the monitoring referred to in paragraph 7.2.2.2 (g) shall be continual. This shall: (a) Include vehicles after first registration in the monitoring

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Mitigate identified cyber threats and vulnerabilities within a reasonable timeframe based on risk categorization.
evidence_quote: The vehicle manufacturer shall demonstrate that the processes used within their Cyber Security Management System will ensure that, based on categorization referred to in paragraph 7.2.2.2 (c) and 7.2.2.2 (g), cyber threats and vulnerabilities which require a response from the vehicle manufacturer shall be mitigated within a reasonable timeframe.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Perform appropriate and sufficient testing prior to type approval to verify the effectiveness of implemented security measures.
evidence_quote: The vehicle manufacturer shall perform, prior to type approval, appropriate and sufficient testing to verify the effectiveness of the security measures implemented.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Implement measures to detect and prevent cyber-attacks and provide data forensic capability for analysis.
evidence_quote: The vehicle manufacturer shall implement measures for the vehicle type to: (a) Detect and prevent cyber-attacks against vehicles of the vehicle type; (c) Provide data forensic capability to enable analysis of attempted or successful cyber-attacks.

source_name: UN Regulation No. 155 — Cybersecurity and Cybersecurity Management Systems (CSMS)
requirement: Ensure cryptographic modules used are in line with consensus standards or justify their use if not.
evidence_quote: Cryptographic modules used for the purpose of this Regulation shall be in line with consensus standards. If the cryptographic modules used are not in line with consensus standards, then the vehicle manufacturer shall justify their use.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Develop, document, and periodically update system security plans describing boundaries, environments, and security implementation.
evidence_quote: Develop, document, and periodically update system security plans that describe system boundaries, system environments of operation, how security requirements are implemented, and the relationships with or connections to other systems.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational systems throughout the system development life cycles.
evidence_quote: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational systems (including hardware, software, firmware, and documentation) throughout the respective system development life cycles.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes to organizational systems.
evidence_quote: Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes to organizational systems.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Establish and manage cryptographic keys for cryptography employed in organizational systems.
evidence_quote: Establish and manage cryptographic keys for cryptography employed in organizational systems.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Establish an operational incident-handling capability including preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities.
evidence_quote: Establish an operational incident-handling capability for organizational systems that includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Track, document, and report incidents to designated officials and/or authorities both internal and external to the organization.
evidence_quote: Track, document, and report incidents to designated officials and/or authorities both internal and external to the organization.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Develop and implement plans of action designed to correct deficiencies and reduce or eliminate vulnerabilities in organizational systems.
evidence_quote: Develop and implement plans of action designed to correct deficiencies and reduce or eliminate vulnerabilities in organizational systems.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Inform managers, systems administrators, and users of organizational systems of the security risks associated with their activities and applicable policies.
evidence_quote: Inform managers, systems administrators, and users of organizational systems of the security risks associated with their activities and of the applicable policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of those systems.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Train personnel to carry out their assigned information security-related duties and responsibilities.
evidence_quote: Train personnel to carry out their assigned information security-related duties and responsibilities.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access to organizational systems containing CUI.
evidence_quote: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access to organizational systems containing CUI.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Protect organizational systems containing CUI during and after personnel actions such as terminations and transfers.
evidence_quote: Protect organizational systems containing CUI during and after personnel actions such as terminations and transfers.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Periodically assess the risk to organizational operations, assets, and individuals resulting from the operation of organizational systems.
evidence_quote: Periodically assess the risk to organizational operations (including mission, functions, image, or reputation), organizational assets, and individuals, resulting from the operation of organizational systems and the associated processing, storage, or transmission of CUI.

source_name: Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) Model Overview v2.13
requirement: Document or reference in the system security plan the security solution selected, the rationale, and the risk determination.

source_name: Leighton-Micali Hash-Based Signatures
requirement: Enforce single-use policy for private key states; once a specific private key value is used to sign a message, it MUST NOT be used to sign another message.
evidence_quote: Once a particular value of the private key is used to sign one message, it MUST NOT be used to sign another.

source_name: Hash-based Signatures: State and Backup Management
requirement: Implement automated state synchronization and integrity checks to prevent OTS key reuse across distributed signers or after backup restoration.
evidence_quote: If the Stateful HBS private key is distributed to multiple signers at the same time, implementers must ensure that they never use the same OTS key. This may require synchronization between all signers.

source_name: Hash-based Signatures: State and Backup Management
requirement: Enforce strict backup management procedures that guarantee restored state does not lead to the reuse of previously consumed One-Time Signature keys.
evidence_quote: If key backups are required, implementers must ensure that any backup mechanism can not lead to re-using a previously used OTS key.

source_name: Hash-based Signatures: State and Backup Management
requirement: Monitor and verify that the state counter is correctly updated after every signature generation to detect and prevent state drift or failure.
evidence_quote: Implementers must ensure that each creation of a signature updates the state correctly.

source_name: Hash-based Signatures: State and Backup Management
requirement: Define operational policies restricting Stateful HBS deployment to environments with tight control, such as hardware security modules, due to state management risks.
evidence_quote: It seems likely that in many scenarios, it is only possible to meet the requirements set out in Section 4 when using purpose-designed hardware, such as hardware-security modules.

requirement: Implement procedures for the generation, establishment, entry, output, storage, and zeroization of Sensitive Security Parameters (SSPs).

requirement: Conduct pre-operational and conditional self-tests to verify the integrity of critical functions and cryptographic operations.

source_name: Recommendation for Key-Derivation Methods in Key-Establishment Schemes (Revision 2)
requirement: Ensure implementations of specified key-derivation functions undergo conformance testing within the CAVP and CMVP frameworks to validate cryptographic correctness.
evidence_quote: Conformance testing for implementations of the functions that are specified in this publication will be conducted within the framework of the Cryptographic Algorithm Validation Program (CAVP) and the Cryptographic Module Validation Program (CMVP).

source_name: Recommendation for Key-Derivation Methods in Key-Establishment Schemes (Revision 2)
requirement: Define and agree upon the content, format, length, and generation method for any auxiliary shared secrets used in hybrid key-establishment schemes before deriving keying material.
evidence_quote: The content, format, length, and method used to generate T must be known and agreed upon by all parties that will rely upon the derived keying material, as well as by any agents trusted to act on their behalf.

source_name: Recommendation for Key-Derivation Methods in Key-Establishment Schemes (Revision 2)
requirement: Restrict the usage of derived keying material to secret keying material purposes such as encryption or integrity, and prohibit its use as a stream cipher key stream.
evidence_quote: The keying material derived using these methods ... shall only be used as secret keying material ... The derived keying material shall not be used as a key stream for a stream cipher.

source_name: Transitioning the Use of Cryptographic Algorithms and Key Lengths (Rev 3)
requirement: Establish documented key-management procedures and policies to define appropriate cryptographic usage and plan for algorithm transitions.
evidence_quote: NIST provides cryptographic key management guidance for defining and implementing appropriate key-management procedures... and planning for possible changes in the use of cryptography

source_name: Transitioning the Use of Cryptographic Algorithms and Key Lengths (Rev 3)
requirement: Implement procedures to transition to stronger cryptographic keys and retire deprecated algorithms according to the defined schedule.
evidence_quote: This publication provides guidance for transitions to the use of stronger cryptographic keys and more robust algorithms.

source_name: Transitioning the Use of Cryptographic Algorithms and Key Lengths (Rev 3)
requirement: Plan for the retirement of specific algorithms (ECB, DSA, SHA-1) and transition to quantum-resistant algorithms as specified.
evidence_quote: This revision proposes a) the retirement of ECB... and the use of DSA... and b) a schedule for the retirement of SHA-1... transition... to quantum-resistant algorithms

source_name: ITU-T X.509 (2019): Public-key and attribute certificate frameworks (ISO/IEC 9594-8)
